info.name: 311 Cases [vw6y-z8j6]
info.description: ## Description SUMMARY Dataset contains SF311 cases created since 7/1/2008 with location information. UPDATE PROCESS This dataset updates daily. HOW TO USE THIS DATASET Download data using 'export' button to the right. For more information on this dataset, visit the FAQ page. Want to map the data in your neighborhood? Check out https://sf.connect.socrata.com/ For more information about Open311, see http://www.open311.org/.

query.description: The serialization format.

request.description:
## Croissant

Fetches dataset metadata conforming to the MLCommons Croissant specification (as JSON-LD).

The Croissant standard ensures rich, interoperable metadata for machine learning datasets.

**Learn more about Croissant:**
* Overview: [https://mlcommons.org/working-groups/data/croissant/](https://mlcommons.org/working-groups/data/croissant/)
* Specification & Tools: [https://github.com/mlcommons/croissant](https://github.com/mlcommons/croissant)

query.description: The serialization format.

request.description:
## DCAT

Fetches dataset metadata conforming to the W3C Data Catalog Vocabulary (DCAT) Version 3 specification.

DCAT is an RDF vocabulary designed to enable standardized descriptions of datasets and services within data catalogs, enhancing discoverability and federation.

**Reference:**
* [Official W3C DCAT 3 Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dcat-3/)

query.description: The serialization format.

request.description:
## DCAT

Fetches dataset metadata conforming to the W3C Data Catalog Vocabulary (DCAT) Version 3 specification.

DCAT is an RDF vocabulary designed to enable standardized descriptions of datasets and services within data catalogs, enhancing discoverability and federation.

**Reference:**
* [Official W3C DCAT 3 Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dcat-3/)

## DDI Codebook

Retrieves dataset metadata structured according to the DDI-Codebook (Version 2.5) specification.

This XML-based standard provides a detailed, machine-readable format for documenting survey data (variables, methodology, etc.) for exchange or archiving, primarily within social sciences.

**Reference:**
* [DDI Codebook 2.5 Specification](https://ddialliance.org/Specification/DDI-Codebook/2.5/)

query.description: The serialization format.

request.description:
## DDI-CDI CDIF

Returns metadata structured according to the DDI Cross-Domain Integration (DDI-CDI) model, using the CODATA Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework (CDIF) recommended profile.

DDI-CDI is designed to facilitate the integration and interoperability of data from diverse sources (e.g., surveys, administrative data, big data). 
CDIF provides a concrete serialization format for this model.

**References:**
* [DDI-CDI Overview (DDI Alliance)](https://ddialliance.org/ddi-cdi)
* [CODATA Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework  (CDIF) Details (CODATA)](https://cdif.codata.org/)

query.description: The serialization format.

request.description:
## DDI-CDI CDIF

Returns metadata structured according to the DDI Cross-Domain Integration (DDI-CDI) model, using the CODATA Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework (CDIF) recommended profile.

DDI-CDI is designed to facilitate the integration and interoperability of data from diverse sources (e.g., surveys, administrative data, big data). 
CDIF provides a concrete serialization format for this model.

**References:**
* [DDI-CDI Overview (DDI Alliance)](https://ddialliance.org/ddi-cdi)
* [CODATA Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework  (CDIF) Details (CODATA)](https://cdif.codata.org/)

item.name: Metadata
item.description:
Metadata is essential for understanding datasets and enabling machine-actionable insights that drive automation, machine learning, and efficient data governance. High-quality, standardized metadata reduces the burden of data wrangling, facilitating faster, more reliable analysis and enabling seamless integration of datasets across platforms.

The requests in this folder retrieve structured metadata for datasets, adhering to several key industry standards. Each standard caters to different needs and contexts:

* **[Croissant](https://mlcommons.org/working-groups/data/croissant/)**: A modern specification from MLCommons designed to describe datasets for **machine learning**, making ML workflows more efficient and reproducible.
* **[DCAT](https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dcat-3/)**: The W3C's Data Catalog Vocabulary (version 3), widely used for **data discovery and cataloging**, especially in government and open data portals.
* **[DDI-Codebook](https://ddialliance.org/Specification/DDI-Codebook/2.5/)**: An established XML-based standard (DDI Version 2.x) from the DDI Alliance, primarily used for documenting **survey data** in the social, behavioral, and economic sciences.
* **[DDI-CDI](https://ddialliance.org/ddi-cdi)**: A newer DDI Alliance standard (DDI Cross-Domain Integration) providing a conceptual model to enable metadata **interoperability across diverse data sources** (e.g., integrating survey data with administrative or sensor data).

By leveraging these standards, the metadata provided supports the **[FAIR data principles](https://www.go-fair.org/fair-principles/)** (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable) and aligns with initiatives like the **[CODATA Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework (CDIF)](https://cdif.codata.org/)**.

These API endpoints are hosted by the **[High-Value Data Network](https://www.highvaluedata.net)** to facilitate broad, cross-functional data utility and understanding.

query.description: The set of columns to be returned, similar to a SELECT in SQL. Default: All columns, equivalent to $select=*.
Filters the rows to be returned, similar to WHERE. No default value.
Column to order results on, similar to ORDER BY in SQL. Default is unspecified order.
Column to group results on, similar to GROUP BY in SQL. Default is no grouping.
Filters the rows that result from an aggregation, similar to HAVING. Default is no filter.
Maximum number of results to return. Default is 1,000. Maximum is 50,000).
Offset count into the results to start at, used for paging. Default is 0.
Performs a full text search for a value. Default is no search.
A full SoQL query string, all as one parameter.
Prepends a UTF-8 Byte Order Mark to the beginning of CSV output. Default is False

item.name: Code Snippets
item.description: Reduce boilerplate code and cut down on data wrangling time using the API requests in this folder. These endpoints generate reusable, customizable code snippets designed to streamline common development and data science tasks. Accelerate your workflows by quickly implementing standard patterns for data querying, metadata API interaction, and boosting overall project productivity.

item.name: SQL
item.description: This folder contains requests to generate SQL code for loading the dataset in various database environments.

request.description:
## Markdown

Returns comprehensive information about a specific dataset, rendered in Markdown format. This output is suitable for general documentation purposes and as structured knowledge or instructions for LLM-based systems and agents.

exec: // Function to run after the response is received
// Set up the HTML template with Showdown library from CDN
font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;
font-family: SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;

item.name: AI
item.description: This folder provides requests aimed at simplifying the use of this dataset within AI platforms, supporting FAIR principles. Access AI-ready metadata and potentially formatted data excerpts to reduce data wrangling and accelerate integration with machine learning models, LLMs, and intelligent agents.

item.name: Visualization
item.description: This folder contains requests to facilitate the visualization of the dataset.
